name: Mrs. Morrison's Mace Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a saucepan combine milk and butter. Bring to a boil and remove from heat.
3. Cream together the eggs and 2 cups sugar in a mixing bowl.
4. Sift together the flour, baking powder, salt, and 1 tablespoon mace.
5. Mix the dry ingredients into the creamed mixture. Add the milk and butter while still hot and mix well.
6. Pour into a greased and floured 9-by-13-inch pan. For the topping, mix the remaining 1/2 cup sugar and 1/2 teaspoon mace together and then sprinkle the mixture over the batter.
7. Bake for 25 to 35 minutes. Alternatively, bake in a 10-inch tube pan at 325°F for 1 hour.
Notes:
This cake was revered by each succeeding congregation at an Episcopal church where Mrs. Morrison served as houskeeper for two to three generations. It is especially suitable for funerals. The wife of the priest notes that it can be quite addictive, and consuming the entire cake may prevent you from completing other tasks for at least 24 hours.
